content.problemStatement:
You’re working on a leaderboard feature for a quiz app. The scores of users are stored in a `HashMap<String, Integer>`, where the key is the username and the value is their score.

Your task is to write a program that **sorts this map by values in descending order** (i.e., highest score first), and prints each key-value pair on a new line in the format:

`username: score`


Input Format

First line: An integer `N`, the number of entries in the map.
Next `N` lines: Each line contains a `username` and `score`, separated by a space.


Output Format

Print the sorted key-value pairs (by score descending), one per line, in the format:

`username: score`
constraints: Usernames contain only lowercase letters and have length ≤ 100
All usernames within a test case are distinct
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000), the number of entries. The next N lines each contain a username (lower‑case letters, length ≤ 100, no spaces) and an integer score (0 ≤ score ≤ 10^6) separated by a single space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the N username‑score pairs sorted by score in descending order. Each pair is printed on its own line in the format "username: score". The outputs of consecutive test cases follow directly without blank lines.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All four entries are sorted by score descending, producing the shown order.
Scores 200, 100 and 50 lead to the descending order displayed.
Higher score (20) comes before lower score (10).

solution.approach: For each test case read all (username, score) pairs into a list/array. Sort the list using a comparator that orders by score in descending order. After sorting, iterate over the list and print each pair in the required "username: score" format. The algorithm is dominated by the sort step, which is O(N log N). Memory usage is O(N) for storing the pairs.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) – sorting each test case of size N.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the array of pairs.
